L1.area: SQL query construction and data persistence utilities

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied identifiers such as table names and column names are directly interpolated into dynamically constructed SQL query strings without sanitization, allowing an attacker to manipulate the resulting queries.

L3.area: SQL query builder utilities for insert, update, and delete operations
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied identifiers such as table names and column names are directly interpolated into dynamically constructed insert, update, and delete SQL query strings without proper escaping or parameterization.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled table names, column names, and ID key names are directly interpolated into SQL query strings via template literals without any sanitization, allowing SQL injection attacks.
